channel.title: Data-Engineering on jamesm.blog
channel.description: Recent content in Data-Engineering on jamesm.blog

item.title: Real-Time Data Processing: Stream Processing vs Batch Processing
item.description: A direct comparison of stream and batch processing as architectural choices, the operational and cost tradeoffs that the marketing material does not mention, and the patterns that work when you need both.

item.title: The Modern Lakehouse Stack: What Actually Belongs in Production
item.description: A working data engineer's view of what genuinely belongs in a 2026 lakehouse stack, the layers that have shaken out, and the parts of the marketing landscape that deserve to be ignored.

item.title: AI-Native Pipelines - What Changes When Your Consumer Is an LLM, Not a Dashboard
item.description: Most data pipelines were designed to feed dashboards and analysts. In 2026 a growing share of pipeline output is being consumed by language models, agents, and other non-human readers. The shape of the pipeline changes more than people realise when the consumer is no longer a human.

item.title: Iceberg vs Delta vs Hudi in 2026 - The Format Wars Are Over
item.description: The open table format wars dominated the lakehouse conversation for five years. In 2026 the picture has settled. A grounded look at where Iceberg, Delta, and Hudi actually stand, who won which battles, and what that means for the people who have to choose.

item.title: The Catalog Layer Is the New Battleground - Unity, Polaris, Gravitino, Nessie
item.description: The open table format wars are over. The fight has moved up to the catalog layer where Unity, Polaris, Gravitino, and Nessie are now competing. A grounded look at what each one is, what they share, and where the battle lines actually run.

item.title: Agent-First Architecture: The Engineer as System Curator
item.description: Some thoughts on where agent-first architecture might take us, and what engineering could look like if the job shifts from building systems line by line to curating, governing, and evolving fleets of agents.

item.title: Apache Iceberg in 2026: The Open Table Format That Won
item.description: A technical deep dive into Apache Iceberg in 2026 - how the metadata layer works, catalog choices (Polaris, Nessie, Unity), migration patterns, and why both Snowflake and Databricks now treat it as first-class.

item.title: Following the Money: Databricks vs Snowflake vs the Open-Source Alternative
item.description: Deep dive into the shifting economics of the data landscape in 2026: why the choice between Snowflake and Databricks is increasingly an accounting decision, and where the open-source DIY stack saves money.

item.title: Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines: From DLT to Production
item.description: From Delta Live Tables to Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines: how declarative ETL patterns have evolved and how to design production pipelines in 2026.

item.title: Modern Data Engineering on Databricks (2026 Guide)
item.description: A 2026 guide to modern data engineering on Databricks, including Unity Catalog, Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, liquid clustering, predictive optimization, and serverless workflows.

item.title: Data Engineering Blogs
item.description: Curated list of Data Engineering blogs covering ETL/ELT, data warehousing, orchestration, and modern data stack best practices.

item.title: Databricks vs Snowflake in 2026: An Honest Comparison
item.description: Head-to-head comparison of Databricks and Snowflake in 2026 - when you should choose each, their actual strengths and weaknesses, and the direction each is moving.

item.title: Data Engineering & Data Science Courses
item.description: Comprehensive curated list of data engineering and data science courses from Coursera, DataCamp, A Cloud Guru, Udacity, Google Cloud, and other platforms with learning paths and certifications.
